recommendations.name: AWS Batch
recommendations.description: Fully managed batch processing at any scale
recommendations.considerations: For your ETL use cases, you might consider using AWS Glue. For other batch-oriented use cases, including some ETL use cases, AWS Batch might be a better fit

recommendations.name: Amazon Aurora for MySQL
recommendations.description: Designed for unparalleled high performance and availability at global scale with full MySQL and PostgreSQL compatibility
recommendations.considerations: Depending on your requirements on resiliency, fault tolerance, performance and dynamic scalability, you may prefer Amazon Aurora to RDS

recommendations.name: Amazon EC2
recommendations.description: Secure and resizable compute capacity in the cloud. Launch applications when needed without upfront commitments

recommendations.name: Amazon EMR
recommendations.description: Easily Run and Scale Apache Spark, Hadoop, HBase, Presto, Hive, and other Big Data Frameworks

recommendations.name: Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS)
recommendations.description: Highly secure, reliable, and scalable way to run containers
recommendations.considerations: The applicability of this recommendation will depend on your organization's container strategy, and which target is recommended as part of this strategy: Virtual machines, Individual containers, Kubernetes clusters, etc. Your container strategy will also inform how processes and people impact the technology choices, if for example a DevOps strategy or a Microservices strategy exists.

recommendations.name: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
recommendations.description: Managed service to start, run, and scale Kubernetes
recommendations.considerations: The applicability of this recommendation will depend on your organization's container strategy, and which target is recommended as part of this strategy: Virtual machines, Individual containers, Kubernetes clusters, etc. Your container strategy will also inform how processes and people impact the technology choices, if for example a DevOps strategy or a Microservices strategy exists.

recommendations.name: Amazon RDS for MySQL
recommendations.description: Set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the cloud with just a few clicks
recommendations.considerations: Depending on your requirements on resiliency, fault tolerance, performance and dynamic scalability, you may prefer Amazon Aurora to RDS

recommendations.name: Amazon RDS for Oracle
recommendations.description: Set up, operate, and scale an Oracle database in the cloud with just a few clicks

recommendations.name: Amazon S3
recommendations.description: Object storage built to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere

recommendations.name: Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS
recommendations.description: Managed OpenShift integration in the cloud. Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS (ROSA) provides an integrated experience with OpenShift. You can use the wide range of AWS compute, database, analytics, machine learning (ML), networking, mobile, and other services to build secure and scalable applications faster.

recommendations.name: Azure Red Hat OpenShift
recommendations.description: Build, deploy, and scale apps on OpenShift with confidence. Azure Red Hat OpenShift provides highly available, fully managed OpenShift clusters on demand, monitored and operated jointly by Microsoft and Red Hat. Kubernetes is at the core of Red Hat OpenShift. OpenShift brings added-value features to complement Kubernetes, making it a turnkey container platform as a service (PaaS) with a significantly improved developer and operator experience.

documentation.title: Intro to Azure Red Hat OpenShift

recommendations.name: Cloud SQL for MySQL
recommendations.description: Cloud SQL for MySQL is a fully-managed database service that helps you set up, maintain, manage, and administer your MySQL relational databases on Google Cloud Platform.

recommendations.name: Cloud Spanner
recommendations.description: Fully managed relational database with unlimited scale, strong consistency, and up to 99.999% availability.

recommendations.name: Cloud Storage
recommendations.description: Object storage for companies of all sizes. Store any amount of data. Retrieve it as often as you’d like.

recommendations.name: Compute Engine
recommendations.description: Accelerate your digital transformation with high-performance VMs.

documentation.title: Choosing the right virtual machine type
Microsoft and Windows on Google Cloud

recommendations.name: Dataproc
recommendations.description: Dataproc is a fully managed and highly scalable service for running Apache Spark, Apache Flink, Presto, and 30+ open source tools and frameworks. Use Dataproc for data lake modernization, ETL, and secure data science, at planet scale, fully integrated with Google Cloud, at a fraction of the cost.

recommendations.name: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE Autopilot)
recommendations.description: GKE Autopilot mode is a hands-off, fully managed solution that manages your entire cluster’s infrastructure without worrying about configuring and monitoring nodes, while still delivering a complete Kubernetes experience.

recommendations.name: Autonomous Database

recommendations.description: Container Instance is a serverless compute service for running containers in a simple, quick, and secure way without managing any infrastructure

recommendations.name: File Storage
recommendations.description: Managed network file system to provide scalable, distributed, enterprise-grade storage. Typically supports NFS

recommendations.name: MySQL Heatwave
recommendations.description: Managed service to create a centralized, curated, and secured repository of raw and transformed data in preparation for analytics

recommendations.name: Oracle Base Database Service
recommendations.description: Oracle Base Database Service enables you to maintain absolute control over your data while leveraging the combined capabilities of Oracle Database and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). OCI offers single-node DB systems and multi-node RAC DB systems on virtual machines

documentation.title: Documentation
